title: Unit, Integration, Fuzz, Race, Leak, and CLI Behavior Evidence · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed ranger review reconstructs unit, integration, fuzz, race, leak, and cli behavior evidence from revision, command, arguments, request identity, response fixture, state transition, cache decision, goroutine ownership, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layered deterministic Go verification harness after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Assign pure domain, handler, cache, transport, persistence, subprocess, race, fuzz, and controlled live-smoke evidence to separate risks.
Use named table cases with minimal original fixtures, changed inputs, explicit wants, and isolated setup without loop-variable mistakes.
Prefer narrow fakes for decisions and httptest for real HTTP boundaries while asserting requests, responses, lifecycle, and faults.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run race-capable suites, force overlapping work, track completion, and test built-command streams, status, signals, working directory, and installed behavior.

At Pokedex product engineering and Go Record what required effort · Review Pokedex Go Race Leak Cli, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 3245 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate layered unit, HTTP, CLI, fuzz, race, and leak evidence on changed behavior; the release claim must identify the exact source and artifact revision; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run race-capable suites, force overlapping work, track completion, and test built-command streams, status, signals, working directory, and installed behavior.

For Pokedex product engineering and Go Record what required effort · Review Pokedex Go Race Leak Cli, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Calling main-like functions directly proves executable lifecycle and concurrency.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
